title: Providing a first person view in a virtual world using a lens
abstract: An interactive virtual world having avatars. Scenes in the virtual world as seen by the eyes of the avatars are presented on user devices controlling the avatars. In one approach, a method includes identifying a location of an avatar in a virtual world, and a point of gaze of the avatar; adjusting, based on the point of gaze, a lens that directs available light received by the lens so that the lens can focus on objects at all distances; collecting, using the adjusted lens, image data; and generating a scene of the virtual world as seen by the avatar, the scene based on the collected image data, the location of the avatar, and the point of gaze of the avatar.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method, comprising: adjusting, by a computing device, a location of an avatar and an orientation of the avatar based on input received in a user device, wherein the location of the avatar changes as the avatar moves through a virtual world, and the orientation includes a point of gaze; identifying, by the computing device, a first location of the avatar in the virtual world, and a first point of gaze of the avatar; adjusting, at least based on the first point of gaze, at least one lens of at least one camera; collecting, using the adjusted lens, image data corresponding to at least one real-world object in a real-world environment; generating a scene of the virtual world as seen by the avatar, the scene based on the collected image data, the first location of the avatar in the virtual world, and the orientation of the avatar in the virtual world; communicating, by the computing device to the user device, the generated scene; and causing the user device to display the generated scene. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the one lens includes a first lens configured to direct available light received by the first lens from the real-world environment to a single focal point, so that the lens can focus on objects at near, intermediate, and far distances in the real-world environment. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the first lens comprises an accommodating posterior chamber. 4. The method of claim 1 , wherein the one lens includes a first lens comprising flexible hinges that enable the first lens to move and flex in response to signals provided by a control system. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the control system includes an eye-tracking system comprising one or more projectors and one or more sensors, wherein 

 the projectors project near-infrared light on one or both eyes of a user of the user device, and the sensors collect image data from the one or both eyes that is processed by an image processor to determine the first point of gaze of the avatar. 6. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: determining, using an eye-tracking system, a second point of gaze of a user of the user device, wherein the eye-tracking system monitors one or more eye movements of the user; and adjusting the point of gaze of the avatar based on the determined second point of gaze of the user. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein a camera includes the one lens and the lens has a field of view, and wherein the camera or the lens rotates in response to changes in the second point of gaze of the user, so that the field of view changes to encompass new objects not visible in the field of view prior to the camera or lens rotating. 8. The method of claim 1 , wherein the generated scene corresponds to a first person view as seen from eyes of the avatar, the method further comprising generating a new scene corresponding to a third person view of at least a portion of the generated scene as seen from a position behind the eyes of the avatar, wherein the new scene is generated in response to input received from the user in the user device. 9. The method of claim 1 , wherein the generated scene includes a virtual scene integrated with a remote location scene, and wherein the remote location scene is based on image data collected using the one lens. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the lens comprises flexible hinges that enable the lens to focus on objects at near, intermediate, and far distances in response to control signals received from the user device. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the control signals are based at least on eye movements detected by an eye-tracking system. 12. The method of claim 1 , wherein the avatar is a first avatar, and the user device is a first user device, the method further comprising: presenting, by the computing device, a second avatar in the virtual world, the second avatar associated with a second user device; receiving, in the computing device, an invitation from the first user device, the invitation identifying the second avatar; communicating, by the computing device to the second device, the invitation; receiving, in the computing device from the second device, an acceptance to the invitation; and teleporting, by the computing device, the second avatar to a vicinity of the first avatar in response to the acceptance. 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising, after teleporting the second avatar: adjusting, by the computing device, a location of the second avatar and an orientation of the second avatar based on input received in the second user device; generating, by the computing device, a second scene of the virtual world as seen by the second avatar according to the location of the second avatar and the orientation of the second avatar, wherein the orientation includes a second point of gaze of the second avatar, and the second scene is generated based at least on image data collected from a lens adjusted based on the second point of gaze; communicating, by the computing device to the second user device, the second scene; and causing the second user device to display the second scene. 14. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: receiving, in the computing device, an image of a user of the user device captured by a camera of the user device; and presenting, using the image, the avatar to second avatars associated with second user devices. 15. The method of claim 1 , wherein the user device is in communication with a sensor of a movement of a hand of a user of the user device, and the location 

 of the avatar is adjusted based on the movement of the hand of the user. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the sensor is mounted on the hand of the user and configured to determine a relative motion between the hand and a display device of the user device. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the user device is in communication with a second sensor configured to detect at least one of head movements or eye movements, and wherein the orientation of the avatar is adjusted based on input from the second sensor. 18. The method of claim 1 , wherein the first point of gaze is identified by an eye-tracking engine, and the scene is generated by a vision engine based on at least one output from an artificial intelligence model. 19. A non-transitory computer-readable medium storing instructions that, when executed by a server computing device, cause the server computing device to: adjust a location of an avatar and an orientation of the avatar based on input received in a user device, wherein the location of the avatar changes as the avatar moves through a virtual world, and the orientation includes a point of gaze; identify a first location of the avatar in the virtual world, and a first point of gaze of the avatar; adjust, at least based on the first point of gaze, at least one lens of at least one camera; collect, using the adjusted lens, image data corresponding to at least one real-world object; and generate a scene of the virtual world as seen by the avatar, the scene based on the collected image data, the location of the avatar in the virtual world, and the orientation of the avatar in the virtual world. 20. A computing device, comprising: at least one microprocessor; and memory coupled to the one microprocessor and storing instructions that, when executed by the one microprocessor, cause the computing 

 device to: identify a first location of an avatar in a virtual world, and a first point of gaze of the avatar; adjust, at least based on the first point of gaze, a lens that directs available light received by the lens to a single focal point, so that the lens can focus on objects at all distances; collect, using the adjusted lens, image data; and generate a scene of the virtual world as seen by the avatar, the scene based on the collected image data, the first location of the avatar, and the first point of gaze of the avatar.
